title: Network Security, Attacks, Segmentation, and Hardening · Incident Case: Net Layer2 Attacks
summary: Threat-model and harden enterprise, guest, IoT, OT, management, and remote-access network zones. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net layer2 attacks increment in a defense-in-depth network security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, ARP poisoning or spoofing, rogue DHCP, and their switching impact and evidence.
Enforce trusted, untrusted, screened, guest, BYOD, IoT, IIoT, SCADA, ICS, and OT boundaries with explicit flows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Layer2 Attacks
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Net Layer2 Attacks contract.

content.text: Explain VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, ARP poisoning or spoofing, rogue DHCP, and their switching impact and evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Layer2 Attacks, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Encryption at the application layer prevents every Layer 2 attack. This fails the Net Layer2 Attacks contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: IEEE 802.3 Ethernet Standard (IEEE 802.3-2022) — Controls Ethernet media, link, speed, duplex, and physical-layer terminology.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain VLAN hopping, MAC flooding, ARP poisoning or spoofing, rogue DHCP, and their switching impact and evidence.

steps.title: Build Net Layer2 Attacks evidence

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Layer2 Attacks into observable performance evidence.
